overall_summary: Iron Mountain supports FERC moving the Secretary's proposed ANOPR forward as a framework to provide large data center customers enhanced flexibility for interstate transmission grid interconnection under federal regulation. The filer advocates for cost allocation fairness, adoption of accelerated interconnection processes from MISO and SPP, direct load payment for infrastructure instead of collateral posting, integration of Grid Enhancing Technologies like batteries, and differentiated treatment for extremely large loads above a percentage threshold of host utility peak load.

quotes.text: Iron Mountain largely supports the Commission expeditiously moving the Secretary's proposed ANOPR forward to a NOPR and eventually a FERC order.
quotes.concern: Support for federal ANOPR process and timeline

quotes.text: invoking limited Commission jurisdiction over the interconnection of large loads to the transmission system is in line with recent FERC orders on the interconnection of generation resources and regulating transmission under the Federal Power Act.
quotes.concern: Federal jurisdiction over large load interconnection is consistent with FPA

quotes.text: The proposed ANOPR lays out a framework that, if implemented, would provide Iron Mountain and other similarly situated customers enhanced flexibility when interconnecting to the interstate transmission grid.
quotes.concern: ANOPR provides flexibility for large customer interconnection

quotes.text: If done correctly, facilitating large loads to interconnect under federal regulation would provide efficient and cost-effective processes that result in fair cost allocation for all electric customers.
quotes.concern: Federal regulation should enable fair cost allocation

quotes.text: Iron Mountain supports the proposed ANOPR because it would provide nationwide process standardization and certainty with the potential for speed to power.
quotes.concern: Support for nationwide standardized interconnection process

quotes.text: Focus on cost allocation that is fair for all.

When reviewing the Secretary's proposal, the Commission should take lessons learned from MISO and SPP to expedite generator interconnections, allow cluster studies and study transmission projects across Regional Transmission Organizations (RTOs).
quotes.concern: Apply MISO/SPP accelerated processes to large loads

quotes.text: MISO's recently approved Expedited Resource Addition Study (ERAS) process provides a targeted solution to get needed generation resources interconnected and online to meet the same load challenges as justification for the proposed ANOPR.
quotes.concern: MISO ERAS model demonstrates effectiveness of expedited processes

quotes.text: In many jurisdictions, utilities are requiring large loads to post collateral and other financial instruments for the length of electric service agreements based on the premise that it mitigates risk.
quotes.concern: Current collateral requirements burden large load customers

quotes.text: Allowing large load customers to directly pay for these transmission and generation investments would provide a more efficient use of capital while at the same time also derisking utilities and other load serving entities.
quotes.concern: Direct payment by loads for infrastructure is more efficient than collateral

quotes.text: The proposed ANOPR could be improved by allowing large load customers to pay directly for improvements on the interstate transmission grid, including GETs, instead of having these costs recovered through retail utility minimum demand charges or other tariff mechanisms that limit the utilization of these investments.
quotes.concern: Large loads should pay directly for interstate grid improvements and GETs

quotes.text: there are areas where extremely large loads will fundamentally have significant impacts on the bulk power system and should be treated differently.
quotes.concern: Extremely large loads require differentiated treatment

quotes.text: Iron Mountain would propose there be a different interconnection path for large loads in a single area that are over a percentage threshold of the host utility's peak load or the local balancing authority's capacity.
quotes.concern: Proposal for separate process for loads exceeding utility peak load percentage

bins.name: Federal FERC jurisdiction over large loads
bins.description: Federal FERC jurisdiction over large load interconnection is appropriate under the Federal Power Act and consistent with generation resource interconnection orders.

bins.name: Standardized nationwide interconnection process
bins.description: The ANOPR should move forward to establish nationwide process standardization and certainty for large load interconnection, incorporating lessons from accelerated processes like MISO ERAS and enabling cluster studies across RTOs.

bins.name: Fair cost allocation principles
bins.description: Federal regulation should enable fair cost allocation across customer classes, with large loads paying directly for transmission and generation investments rather than posting collateral, improving capital efficiency and derisking utilities.

bins.name: Enhanced interconnection flexibility
bins.description: The ANOPR framework should provide large customers enhanced flexibility for interstate transmission grid interconnection under federal oversight.

bins.name: MISO ERAS model for expedited interconnection
bins.description: MISO's Expedited Resource Addition Study process demonstrates an effective approach for accelerated interconnections that should be applied to large load customers.

bins.name: SPP lessons for large load processes
bins.description: FERC should incorporate lessons learned from SPP's accelerated interconnection approaches when designing large load interconnection rules.

bins.name: Collateral and financial instruments burden
bins.description: Current requirements by utilities for large loads to post collateral and financial instruments unnecessarily tie up capital that should be deployed to infrastructure and resources.

bins.name: Direct payment by large loads for infrastructure
bins.description: Large load customers should be able to directly pay for transmission and generation investments instead of posting collateral, improving capital efficiency and derisking utilities.

bins.name: Grid Enhancing Technologies integration
bins.description: The ANOPR should facilitate deployment of Grid Enhancing Technologies such as long duration batteries and enable large loads to pay directly for these interstate grid improvements rather than recovering costs through retail tariff mechanisms.

bins.name: Differentiated treatment for extremely large loads
bins.description: Loads exceeding a percentage threshold of host utility peak load or local balancing authority capacity should receive differentiated interconnection treatment due to significant impacts on the bulk power system.
